Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Governor Generalâs Act (retiring annuity and other benefits)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Governor Generalâs Act (retiring annuity and other benefits)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Governor Generalâs Act to provide that a Governor General is not eligible for a retiring annuity or other benefits unless they hold office for at least five consecutive years.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: The title of Part II of the Governor Generalâs Act is replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: Governor Generalâs Retiring Annuity and Other Benefits

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 6(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
When a Governor General who has held office for at least five consecutive years ceases to hold office as such, there shall be paid to him an annuity equal to the aggregate of

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 2: (1)
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Text of the relevant portion of subsection 6(1):

Subsection.Text: Where a Governor General ceases to hold office as such, there shall be paid to him an annuity equal to the aggregate of
The Act is amended by adding the following after subsection 6(1):

A Governor General who, in the opinion of the Governor in Council, is unable for medical reasons to serve for five consecutive years is deemed to have served for at least five consecutive years for the purpose of subsection (1).

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 11:

No money shall be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund for the purpose of providing administrative support to or reimbursing expenses incurred by a former Governor General who held office for less than five consecutive years.

Subsection.Text: The payment, under Part II of the Governor Generalâs Act as it read immediately before the day on which this Act comes into force, of an annuity to a former Governor General who held office for less than five consecutive years ceases on the day on which this Act comes into force.

The payment, under Part II of the Governor Generalâs Act as it read immediately before the day on which this Act comes into force, of an annuity to a survivor shall continue during the life of the survivor.
